libraryDescription:
This library, graphics2d.Graphics2DFramework, is a lightweight framework for handling 2D computer graphics easily.

When writing a 2D CG program from scratch using only the standard VCSSL libraries Graphics2D and GUI,
you need to write several fixed pieces of code every time, such as creating the window and renderer, the animation loop,and basic event handlers.
Graphics2DFramework supports easy 2D CG development by preparing such fixed processing in advance as a framework template.

Required Processing

Structurally, Graphics2DFramework is already a complete 2D CG program by itself, and it can actually run as a standalone program.
However, by itself it has no drawing contents, so only a white screen is displayed.

In practice, you import Graphics2DFramework from your own module and describe the required processing, such as placing and moving sprites.
Such processing is written by defining functions with specific names, just like event handlers for GUI components.
Then Graphics2DFramework automatically executes those event handler functions at appropriate timings.

Graphics2DFramework automatically executes the following event handler functions. Their definitions are optional, so define only the ones you need.

  • void onStart( int renderer ) - Executed only once at startup. Write initialization processing such as creating and placing sprites here. The old name initialize is also supported.
  • void onExit( int renderer ) - Executed only once at shutdown. Write any shutdown processing here if needed. The old name finalize is also supported.
  • void onPaint( int renderer ) - Executed every time the screen is updated, usually dozens of times per second. Write processing here when you want to draw 2D CG contents directly for animation. The old name paint is also supported.
  • void onUpdate( int renderer ) - Executed every time between screen updates, usually dozens of times per second. Write processing here when you want to move sprites and so on. The old name update is also supported.
  • void onResize( int renderer ) - Executed when the window size changes. Write processing here when you want to adjust the layout of GUI components placed on the screen. The old name resize is also supported.
In all of the above functions, the argument renderer receives the ID of the 2D CG renderer. Use it for drawing and for registering sprites.

Window Customization

Graphics2DFramework allows margin areas to be set inside the window, where GUI components such as buttons and text fields can be placed.
Margins can be configured with functions such as setMarginTop ( int margin ) and setMarginLeft ( int margin ).
Then obtain the window ID by getWindow ( ) and place GUI components freely in the margin areas.

Frame Rate Adjustment

Graphics2DFramework also has an automatic frame rate adjustment mechanism.
Specifically, it measures the time required for each frame and automatically adjusts the wait time of the animation loop so that the measured frame rate approaches the configured target frame rate.
Set the target frame rate with setFrameRate ( float rate ).The measured frame rate can be obtained with getCurrentFrameRate ( ).

Slowdown

Depending on the processing speed of the execution environment, if the drawing area is large or the drawing contents are heavy,
the measured frame rate may fail to reach the target frame rate even when the animation wait time is minimized. This phenomenon is a slowdown.

When a slowdown occurs, screen repainting may fail to keep up and the display may flicker.
In that case, flicker can be reduced by setting the target frame rate to a value with enough margin below the measured frame rate limit.
However, because the measured frame rate depends heavily on the environment,flicker can also be reduced by setting a relatively large lower limit for the animation wait time in milliseconds with setAnimationWaitMin ( int wait ).
Note that this lowers the upper limit of the measured frame rate.

function0.name: getGraphics
function0.description: Returns the graphics ID of the 2D CG resource.
function0.returnValue: (int type) The graphics ID of the 2D CG graphics.

function1.name: getRenderer
function1.description: Returns the renderer ID of the 2D CG renderer.
function1.returnValue: (int type) The renderer ID of the 2D CG renderer.

function2.name: getImageLabel
function2.description: Returns the GUI component ID of the image label displaying the drawing contents.
function2.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the image label.

function3.name: getGraphicsLabel
function3.description: Returns the GUI component ID of the image label.The newer getImageLabel function is available for this purpose.This function is supported for compatibility.
function3.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the drawing area label.

function4.name: getWindow
function4.description: Returns the GUI component ID of the window.
function4.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the window.

function5.name: setAnimationState
function5.description: Sets whether animation mode is enabled.
function5.parameters: (bool type) enable : Whether animation mode is enabled. It is enabled when true.

function6.name: getAnimationState
function6.description: Gets whether animation mode is enabled.
function6.returnValue: (bool type) Whether animation mode is enabled. It is enabled when true.

function7.name: setFrameRate
function7.description: Sets the screen update frequency of the animation, that is, the number of redraws per second.
function7.parameters: (float type) rate : The screen update frequency.

function8.name: getFrameRate
function8.description: Gets the configured screen update frequency of the animation, that is, the number of redraws per second.
function8.returnValue: (float type) The configured screen update frequency.

function9.name: getCurrentFrameRate
function9.description: Gets the measured screen update frequency of the animation, that is, the number of redraws per second.
function9.returnValue: (float type) The measured screen update frequency.

function10.name: getLastFrameTime
function10.description: Gets the time of the last screen update of the animation.The returned time usually represents the elapsed time from program startup in milliseconds.However, the unit may differ depending on the implementation.To eliminate implementation dependence completely, convert it through the Time.millisecond function before using it.
function10.returnValue: (int type) The time of the last screen update, usually in milliseconds.

function11.name: setPaintGraphics2DState
function11.description: Sets whether Graphics2D.paintGraphics2D is called automatically when the screen is drawn, before the paint call.If true is specified, it is called automatically.If false is set, it is not called automatically, so it needs to be called explicitly in the paint event handler or elsewhere.

function12.name: getPaintGraphics2DState
function12.description: Gets whether Graphics2D.paintGraphics2D is called automatically when the screen is drawn, before the paint call.

function13.name: setBackgroundColor
function13.declaration: void setBackgroundColor(int r, int g, int b, int a)
function13.description: Sets the background color. Specify the red, green, blue, and alpha components of the background color in the range from 0 to 255.
function13.parameters:
(int type) r : The red component of the background color, from 0 to 255. 
(int type) g : The green component of the background color, from 0 to 255. 
(int type) b : The blue component of the background color, from 0 to 255. 
(int type) a : The alpha component of the background color, from 0 to 255.

function14.name: setBackgroundColor
function14.description: Sets the background color. Specify an array storing the red, green, blue, and alpha components of the background color in the range from 0 to 255.
function14.parameters: (int[] type) color : The background color array, where [0]=red, [1]=green, [2]=blue, and [3]=alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function15.name: getBackgroundColor
function15.description: Gets the background color. The returned value is an array storing the red, green, blue, and alpha components of the background color in the range from 0 to 255.
function15.returnValue: (int[] type) The background color array, where [0]=red, [1]=green, [2]=blue, and [3]=alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function18.name: setWindowSize
function18.parameters:
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height.

function19.name: setWindowSize
function19.parameters: (int[] type) size : The window size, where [0]=width and [1]=height.

function20.name: getWindowSize
function20.returnValue: (int[] type) The window size, where [0]=width and [1]=height.

function21.name: setWindowLocation
function21.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the window.

function22.name: setWindowLocation
function22.parameters: size : The coordinates of the top-left corner of the window, where [0]=X and [1]=Y.

function23.name: getWindowLocation
function23.returnValue: (int[] type) The coordinates of the top-left corner of the window, where [0]=X and [1]=Y.

function24.name: setMarginRight
function24.description: Sets the right margin of the window.
function24.parameters: (int type) margin : The margin in pixels.

function25.name: getMarginRight
function25.description: Gets the right margin of the window.

function26.name: setMarginLeft
function26.description: Sets the left margin of the window.

function27.name: getMarginLeft
function27.description: Gets the left margin of the window.

function28.name: setMarginTop
function28.description: Sets the top margin of the window.

function29.name: getMarginTop
function29.description: Gets the top margin of the window.

function30.name: setMarginBottom
function30.description: Sets the bottom margin of the window.

function31.name: getMarginBottom
function31.description: Gets the bottom margin of the window.

function32.name: setAnimationWaitMin
function32.description: Sets the lower limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function33.name: getAnimationWaitMin
function33.description: Sets the lower limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function34.name: setAnimationWaitMax
function34.description: Sets the upper limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function35.name: getAnimationWaitMax
function35.description: Sets the upper limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function36.name: setMainLoopState
function36.description: Sets whether the main loop continues. If it is set to false, the animation main loop exits and the program ends.
function36.parameters: (bool type) state : Whether the main loop continues.

function37.name: getMainLoopState
function37.description: Gets whether the main loop continues.
function37.returnValue: (bool type) Whether the main loop continues.

function38.name: getMainArgument
function38.description: Gets the arguments of the main function.
function38.returnValue: (string[] type) The arguments of the main function.

function39.name: export
function39.declaration: void export( string filePath, string format, float quality )
function39.description: Outputs the current contents of the drawing label as an image file.File output is not performed at the moment this function is called.It is executed at the next screen update timing.
function39.parameters:
(string type) filePath : The output file name or file path. 
(string type) format : The file format, either "PNG" or "JPEG". 
(float type) quality : The quality.

function40.name: main
function40.description: The main function. It is normally executed automatically and calls the frameworkMain function.However, if the main program defines the main function,this function is no longer executed automatically, sothe frameworkMain function below needs to be called explicitly.
function40.parameters: (string[] type) args : The main arguments, which can also be obtained with the getMainArgument function.

function41.name: frameworkMain
function41.description: The main processing of the framework. It is normally executed automatically from the main function.However, if the main program defines the main function,it is no longer executed automatically, so it needs to be called at an appropriate timing.
function41.parameters: (string[] type) args : The main arguments, which can also be obtained with the getMainArgument function.

function42.name: onWindowResize
function42.declaration: void onWindowResize( int id, int width, int height )
function42.description: The event handler called when the window is resized.
function42.parameters:
(int type) id : The GUI component ID of the window. 
(int type) width : The width of the window. 
(int type) height : The height of the window.

function43.name: onWindowClose
function43.description: The event handler called when the window is closed.
function43.parameters: (int type) id : The GUI component ID of the window.

function44.name: paintWindow
function44.description: Repaints the window.It is called automatically at the screen update timing of the animation, butit can also be called explicitly at any timing to force a repaint.

function45.name: render
function45.description: Calls the paint function and related processing to render the screen contents.It is normally executed automatically, but when animation is disabled and in similar cases,it needs to be called explicitly when the screen is updated.
